newsSince: No new contract announcements in-window, but on June 22 CAT closed above $1,000 for the first time as management flagged a record ~$63B enterprise backlog (up ~79% y/y), driven by data-center Power & Energy demand. The marquee orders (AIP/Boyd 2GW Monarch, Joule/Wheeler 4GW Utah) were announced in January 2026 and predate the window.

sources.label: Caterpillar hits record high on AI data-center power demand
Caterpillar 2GW alliance with AIP / Boyd CAT (Jan 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: record ~$63B backlog (+79% y/y) from data-center gensets is a real fundamental datapoint, but marquee orders predate the window and CAT remains a $457B diversified benchmark name far from the passive bottleneck. Tiny positive for backlog confirmation; hold rank.
verdicts.bull: Record $63B backlog with Power & Energy as lead driver confirms structural AI-genset demand pull-through.
verdicts.neutral: Backlog reflects already-announced orders; mega-cap diversification dilutes the AI-power signal.
verdicts.bear: +66% YTD and record-high price; far from the passive/power bottleneck stack, residual alpha thin.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclicality / mega-cap dilution of AI exposure
